name: vr-org-mcp
description: Read-only access to VR.org's live VR, AR, and XR news, full-text editorial, events, deals, and buyer guides.
long_description:
Read-only Model Context Protocol server that gives AI agents one-call access to VR.org, a dedicated VR / AR / XR news publication and aggregator. Eleven tools, zero keys, zero writes.

News and discovery: search the live aggregated feed of VR, AR, and XR headlines across VR-native and filtered general-tech sources, get currently trending topics, and list the sources VR.org aggregates.

Editorial: list VR.org's original articles (reporting, opinion, retrospectives, guides) and fetch any one by slug, including its full body text.

Events: pull VR.org's calendar of upcoming VR, AR, and XR industry events (conferences, expos, launches), soonest first.

Shopping and guides: get VR.org's curated product deals and prices, compare two headsets side by side, pull the current top-VR-games and top-VR-apps lists, and get canonical short answers with pillar-page links for common questions like what is VR, best headset, and AR glasses.

Every tool is read-only and simply composes VR.org's public JSON API. The server holds no secrets, accepts no payments, and cannot write anything. Outputs are sanitized (control and direction-override characters stripped) and capped at 50 KB so a malicious upstream headline cannot inject content into the calling agent's reasoning or blow out its context window.
